summary: DHH announces that Basecamp is adjusting its pricing for the first time since launching nearly a year earlier. He acknowledges the inherent risk in changing prices once customers have established expectations around the original rates. The team deliberated carefully to make changes as fair and balanced as possible. He expresses hope that existing customers will accept the new structure, enabling Basecamp to grow in 2005.
key_points: Initial pricing creates a baseline reality that becomes very difficult to change later
Price increases must operate within the logic of customers' existing expectations
Deliberate, fair adjustments are key to retaining customer trust during pricing changes
Basecamp was positioning for significant growth heading into 2005
money_quotes: No matter what you initially set your prices at, you've created a baseline and a set of expectations that are very hard to work against.
In short, you've created a pricing reality and the increases needs to play within that logic.
So we deliberated long and hard over how we could make the adjustments as fair and evenly balanced as possible.
key_insight: Once you set a price, you've created a pricing reality that constrains all future adjustments, so initial pricing decisions carry long-term consequences.
